prediction.summary: User emma.hr, who submitted her resignation 48 hours prior, engaged in a series of suspicious activities indicative of data exfiltration. She accessed numerous sensitive HR and legal documents, performed a bulk download of 842MB, copied this data to a removable USB device, and then connected a personal cloud sync client to an external domain. This behavior triggered a DLP policy violation and SIEM alerts for insider threat and data exfiltration.

timeline.event: emma.hr copied 842MB to removable device E:\backup
emma.hr connected personal cloud sync client sync.evil-drop.io
policy violation: HR confidential data staged for external sync by emma.hr
insider threat pattern: bulk sensitive file access + USB + cloud sync
user emma.hr resignation submitted 48 hours ago risk score elevated
MITRE T1530 Data from Cloud Storage / internal collection
emma.hr accessed 47 files outside normal role scope in 54 minutes

recommendations: Immediately disable all network and system access for user `emma.hr`.
Initiate a forensic investigation of `LAPTOP-HR-09` and any associated file shares accessed by `emma.hr` to determine the full scope of data accessed and exfiltrated.
Block access to `sync.evil-drop.io` at the network perimeter and investigate any other connections to this domain.
Review HR and legal policies regarding departing employees and data handling, and reinforce DLP measures.
Notify legal counsel and relevant stakeholders regarding potential data breach.
